text.title: Agent-Specific Rules and AI Gotchas
text.summary: Hard rules for reporting agents, human-in-loop checkpoints, and failure modes that LLMs commonly hit with Google Ads reporting.

rule.statement: Reporting agents must be read-only; mutations (pause, budget change, status flip) require a separate agent with explicit human approval before execution.
rule.rationale: Mixing read and write in one agent increases blast radius. A reporting bug that accidentally triggers a mutation can pause campaigns or deplete budgets before anyone notices.

list.item: Any auto-mutation (pause, budget change, status flip) — even threshold-based — requires human notification before execution
Bid strategy change on any campaign
Adding/removing keywords with spend impact above a defined threshold
Any change that touches more than 10% of active campaigns in one run

antipattern.description: Agent writes cost_micros / 1_000 instead of cost_micros / 1_000_000.
antipattern.reason: Results in costs reported as 1,000x the actual value. Alerts fire on every campaign, budgets appear exhausted, and the bug is not immediately obvious in large number output.

antipattern.description: Agent uses metrics.all_conversions where metrics.conversions is correct.
antipattern.reason: all_conversions includes every conversion action (including micro-conversions); conversions only counts actions marked "include in Conversions." The first inflates ROAS calculations.

antipattern.description: Agent presents impression share as a percentage without multiplying by 100 first.
antipattern.reason: metrics.search_impression_share is a fraction (0.45 = 45%). Displaying the raw value as "45%" is correct; displaying it as "0.45%" is wrong and will cause alarm where none is warranted.

antipattern.description: Agent hallucinates GAQL field names not present in the API (e.g., metrics.spend instead of metrics.cost_micros).
antipattern.reason: The Google Ads API returns a generic error for unknown field names. Agents must validate field names against the API reference before running queries in production.

rule.statement: Always store raw GAQL output to BigQuery or disk before transformation; Google occasionally backfills metrics retroactively and raw snapshots are needed to reproduce historical reports.
rule.rationale: Without raw snapshots, retroactive metric changes produce unexplained discrepancies between current reports and cached dashboards, destroying trust in the data.
